headlines.title: Fake Trump and Oprah ads fuel a wave of Medicare scams on Facebook, report says
headlines.description: Meta, the parent of Facebook and Instagram, is failing to stop scammers who repeatedly buy ads on the platforms to target seniors with scams, a new report says.

headlines.title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella testifies in OpenAI trial
headlines.description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella took the witness stand in a trial about control of the artificial intelligence startup OpenAI.

headlines.title: Lawsuit says ChatGPT told FSU shooter that targeting children would bring more attention
headlines.description: OpenAI faces a lawsuit alleging that ChatGPT played a role in a mass shooting at Florida State University in April 2025 that left two people dead.

headlines.title: OpenAI co-founder says he feared Musk would 'physically attack' him
headlines.description: Co-founder Greg Brockman said he had a tense confrontation with Musk after there was a disagreement about potential equity shares in OpenAI.

headlines.title: Pennsylvania suing AI company after chatbot allegedly posed as a licensed doctor
headlines.description: An artificial intelligence company poses a threat to "vulnerable Pennsylvanians," state officials said after one of the company's chatbots was accused of posing as a doctor with the means to prescribe medication.

headlines.title: April inflation rate surges to 3.8% amid uncertainty in the Middle East
headlines.description: Inflation rose to 3.8% year over year in April and 0.6% month over month as the war in Iran continues to affect the economy. NBC News' Christine Romans and editor-in-chief at Investopedia Caleb Silver report on the economic situation amid uncertainty in the Middle East.

headlines.title: Hegseth and Caine testify at Senate hearing amid Iran war
headlines.description: Watch live coverage as Defense Secretary Hegseth and Joint Chiefs of Staff Chairman Gen. Caine testify at a Senate Appropriations Committee hearing on the Department of Defense's budget request amid the Iran war and uncertainty around a possible peace deal as the cost of the conflict mounts.

headlines.title: Ship operators involved in Baltimore bridge collapse charged with misconduct and obstruction
headlines.description: The Justice Department brought 18 charges against the operators and a manager relating to a 2024 collision with the Francis Scott Key Bridge that killed six people.

headlines.title: Londoners pessimistic about Prime Minister Starmer's future
headlines.description: Londoners were pessimistic over Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s future after his Labour Party suffered heavy losses in local elections across the U.K.

headlines.title: Trump Mobile phone release date still uncertain
headlines.description: Nearly a year has passed since the announcement of the Trump Mobile wireless service, but its release date remains uncertain due to delays. NBC News’ Brian Cheung reports on the phone’s development, and what it means for customers who have paid deposits.

headlines.title: OpenAI sued by family of victim killed in FSU mass shooting
headlines.description: The family of a victim who was killed in a mass shooting at Florida State University in April 2025 is suing OpenAI, saying that ChatGPT gave the suspect advice about how to carry out the attack. NBC News’ Danny Cevallos talks about the new legal frontier of dealing with AI and if the chatbot could be viewed as a co-conspirator in this case.

headlines.title: Schools across U.S. disrupted after Canvas education platform hacked
headlines.description: The parent company of Canvas, a popular, cloud-based education platform, says it experienced a cybersecurity incident this week. NBC News' Valerie Castro explains the impact this has had on students.
